kicker: ET LEVEL I // PRIMARY & SECONDARY FIELDS

beats.narration: Every eddy current test is a conversation between two magnetic fields: one you drive, one the part fires back.

payload.title: TWO FIELDS, ONE COIL
payload.sub: The primary you drive — the secondary the part fires back.

beats.narration: First, the primary field — the one you control. AC through the coil builds a field that expands, collapses, and reverses every cycle.

payload.heading: The primary field: yours to control

beats.narration: Set the coil on metal: Faraday drives eddy currents in closed loops. Their secondary field obeys Lenz — opposing the change. The part pushes back.
That push-back cancels flux, so inductive reactance drops; eddy current heating adds resistance. The coil is a transformer, the part one shorted turn.

payload.heading: What the push-back does to the coil

beats.narration: A crack, conductivity change, or lift-off disturbs the eddy currents, shifts the secondary field, and the instrument reads that as your indication.

payload.sub: The instrument never sees the flaw — only the change.
